summary: Developer Cabinet audit verified — backend AND frontend pass. 15/15 backend pytest cases green covering all four /api/developer/support-tickets endpoints (list/create/detail/respond) + validation (empty title=400, bad type=400, bad priority=400, 404 on missing ticket, empty-message reply=400, auth gating 401/403 without token) + three /api/notifications/* endpoints (unread-count, my, mark-read). Frontend E2E (mobile viewport 390x844, John developer token seeded into localStorage atlas_token/atlas_user) confirmed: (1) /developer/home renders Execution Hub with notification-bell visible top-right; (2) tapping bell routes to /developer/notifications which renders developer-notifications screen with all four filter pills (all/unread/work/money) and notifications-empty empty-state since John has 0 unread; (3) /developer/profile shows the three new Support-section rows ('Support tickets', 'Live chat with admin', 'Notifications') by text match; (4) /developer/support renders developer-support screen, 'New' button expands support-form, type chip (bug), priority chip (high), title-input + desc-input fill correctly, submit POSTs to /api/developer/support-tickets returning 200 and the ticket list refreshes to 4 cards (3 prior + 1 new E2E ticket); (5) tapping a ticket card opens /developer/support/{ticket_id} thread with reply composer (testID ticket-reply-input) and 'CONVERSATION (n)' header rendering correctly; (6) 'Live chat with admin' shortcut row (support-open-chat) routes to /chat; (7) /developer/module/{invalid_id} renders the graceful 'Module not found — Back to Home' empty state (correctly handles 404 from /api/developer/why-assigned/{id}). Backend logs confirm round-trip 200s; no red screen, no Module-not-found bundler error, no PageError.

react_native_issues.issue: Console shows 'React has detected a change in the order of Hooks called by %s. This will lead to bugs and errors if not fixed.' on multiple developer route loads (home, notifications, profile, support). Not blocking any flow today, but indicates a conditional-hook somewhere in the developer surface tree (possibly _layout.tsx or notification-bell.tsx) and should be located/fixed before it manifests as a real stale-render bug.

frontend_e2e_flows: Submit ticket (POST /api/developer/support-tickets) returns 200 + list refresh — PASS
Tap ticket card → /developer/support/{id} thread with reply input — PASS
Live chat with admin row → /chat — PASS
/developer/module/{invalid_id} → graceful 'Module not found' empty state — PASS
frontend_e2e_flows_not_executed: /developer/home → tap dev-home-module-* (testID): John developer has 0 active modules in current DB state (Execution Hub: 'ACTIVE MODULES (0/0)'), so the testID-prefixed module cards cannot be tapped end-to-end. Direct navigation to /developer/module/{invalid_id} confirms screen renders + 404 path is handled. To complete this verification main agent should either (a) seed a module assigned to user_23ce824521df, or (b) accept an open module from /developer/market first.

Submit-for-QA modal URL validation (https-only) and Drop-module Alert.confirm: blocked by the same '0 active modules' state above. Backend endpoint contracts (POST /api/modules/{id}/submit + POST /api/marketplace/modules/{id}/drop) were not exercised this iteration.

action_items: [LOCATE & FIX] React Hooks-order warning on /developer/* routes. Search likely culprits: src/notification-bell.tsx (early-return before hooks?), app/developer/_layout.tsx, app/developer/module/[id].tsx (the new modal state may be after a conditional return). The warning fires on every developer screen load.

[MISSING testIDs in /developer/profile.tsx] Only 'profile-notifications' was found. Add explicit testIDs to the Support-tickets and Live-chat rows for E2E coverage: 'profile-support-tickets' and 'profile-live-chat' (or whatever naming pattern matches the existing profile testID convention).

[SEED NEEDED for full module/[id] E2E] To verify module detail screen interactions (Submit-for-QA modal with URL validation, Drop confirmation, View QA feedback, Message admin), seed at least 1 active module assigned to user_23ce824521df (john@atlas.dev). Without this, the dev-home-module-* tap → /developer/module/{real_id} → submit/drop flows cannot be executed end-to-end. Backend round-trip on the new submit/drop endpoints remains unverified this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: developer_support.py is clean, focused, and mirrors client_support correctly: same backing collections, audience='developer' marker for admin queue source-of-truth, idempotent POST (no duplicate ticket created when frontend retries with same Idempotency-Key header — runtime client handles dedup), reopen-on-reply behavior matches client flow. Validation (VALID_TYPES, VALID_PRIORITIES, empty-title/message guards) is consistent with the rest of the support stack.

support.tsx + notifications.tsx + support/[id].tsx use the bare-prefix runtime.<m>() convention (e.g., runtime.get('/api/developer/support-tickets')) which is consistent with Batch-5 web-client migration completed in iter_5. No axios residue.

module/[id].tsx uses TextInput inside a Modal/View for the Submit-for-QA URL prompt (NOT Alert.prompt), which is the correct cross-platform choice per the agent-to-agent note. Web preview confirmed renders without red-screen.
Profile section visibility verified by innerText match in DOM — all three rows render. Only quibble is the testID gap (see action item 2).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_developer_support_tickets.py (NEW — 15-case suite covering developer-support CRUD + validation + auth gating + notifications endpoints)

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 PASS — list/create/detail/respond + 5 validation cases + 3 notifications endpoints + 2 auth gating cases)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on executed flows (8/8 PASS). 3 sub-flows (module-card tap + Submit-for-QA modal + Drop confirmation) NOT EXECUTED due to environmental state: developer has 0 active modules. Workaround verified: nonexistent module ID renders graceful 'Module not found' empty state.

seed_data_creation: Created 1 E2E test ticket (title 'E2E ticket {hex6}') via /developer/support form submit. Backend pytest TestDeveloperSupportTickets also created 1 'TEST_ ticket {hex6}' (via POST) and 1 'TEST_ reply {hex6}' (via respond). Total tickets in John's support inbox now: 4. No cleanup performed — main agent may safely retain or sweep TEST_*/E2E* prefixed rows in support_tickets / ticket_responses collections. NO active module was seeded for user_23ce824521df; see action_item 3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run backend regression: `/root/.venv/bin/python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_developer_support_tickets.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/developer_support_iterN.xml`. Expect 15/15 PASS. For frontend, use the playwright pattern that worked in iter_6: login via page.request.post (NOT urllib.request — the cluster WAF returned 403 for urllib UA), seed atlas_token + atlas_user in localStorage on root '/', then page.goto('/developer/home'). Bell-to-notifications, support flow, profile-support rows, support thread reply, module 404-empty-state are all data-testid-stable and ready for regression. Module/[id] happy-path E2E is gated on a real assigned module — seed it first or wait for John to claim from /developer/market.
rca_of_issue: No production bugs found in the developer-cabinet audit work. Two observations to fix: (a) testID gap on 2 of 3 new profile rows — pure missing-attribute, no functional impact; (b) React Hooks-order warning on developer routes — present BEFORE the audit changes per the existing console pattern (also seen in iter_5 expo log warnings), but agent-to-agent note flagged 'pre-existing TS-parse ESLint warnings are NOT regressions — already there for client/admin layouts'; the React Hooks one is a DIFFERENT warning (runtime, not lint) and should be tracked separately — confirmed it fires on home, notifications, profile, and support route loads, so it is structural in the developer surface and not specific to a single new file.
